zh.lifeSummary: 當John Marshall於1411年出生在英國德比夏爾諾波利時，他的父親William Marshall Of Somercotes32歲，母親Catherine Leeke32歲。他於1429年與Elizabeth Emma Bingham在英國英格蘭諾福爾可辛加姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1460年在英國享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Marshall于1411年出生在英国德比夏爾諾波利时，他的父亲William Marshall Of Somercotes32岁，母亲Catherine Leeke32岁。他于1429年与Elizabeth Emma Bingham在英国英格兰諾福爾可辛加姆结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1460年在英国享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: Marshall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.
Americanized form of German   Marschall  .
Americanized form of French   Mercier  .

variants.name: Marchel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jacek, Ryszard.  German Ernst.

Polish: probably from French maréchal or Old French maresc(h)al, which was originally an occupational name for someone who was in charge of the horses of a household, later a military rank for a senior general. See Marshall and compare Marszalek .

variants.name: Mercier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Maniscalco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Sal, Antonio, Ignazio, Carmelo, Francesco, Gaspare, Saverio, Antonino, Carlo, Damiano.

Italian (southern): occupational name from maniscalco ‘farrier’, altered from marescalco by folk etymology, probably influenced by mano ‘hand’, from medieval Latin mariscalcus, marescalcus. Compare Marshall .

variants.name: Marchal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Jacques, Maryse, Philippe, Pierre, Veronique.

French and Walloon: status name or occupational name from Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The term is of ancient Germanic origin (from marah ‘horse, mare’ + scalc ‘servant’) and was originally applied to a man who looked after horses. In the Middle Ages it also came to be used on the one hand as an occupational name for a farrier, and on the other as a status name for an officer of state, in particular a member of a royal household with military responsibilities. Compare English Marshall and 2 below.
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland, and is found mainly in Glamorgan, Wales.

variants.name: Maskell
meanings: English: variant of Marshall , derived from Middle English, Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The first element in the placename Mascallsbury (Essex) derives from the surname of a 13th-century individual named Robert le Marescal.

variants.name: Marszalek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Piotr, Alicja, Andrzej, Eugeniusz, Henryk, Jerzy, Kazimierz, Mieczyslaw, Wieslaw, Zbigniew, Zofia, Zygmunt.

Polish (Marszałek): occupational or status name from the vocabulary word marszałek ‘marshal’, denoting anything from a high court official, a field marshal, or the chairman of the Polish parliament, to the senior servant in a great household. Compare English Marshall .

variants.name: Marshell
meanings: English: variant of   Marshall  .

variants.name: Marschall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Hedwig, Juergen, Reinhard, Theresia, Wilfried.

German: occupational name for a man in the service of a member of the nobility, originally one who looked after the horses, Middle High German marschalc (from mar(c) ‘noble horse’ + schalk ‘servant’). In time the term came to be used for a military rank or a certain court official. Compare Marshall .
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marshal
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Marshall  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Marshall was born in 1411, in Norbury, Derbyshire, England, his father, William Marshall Of Somercotes, was 32 and his mother, Catherine Leeke, was 32. He married Elizabeth Emma Bingham in 1429, in Hingham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1460, in England, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Marshall est né en 1411, à Norbury, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, William Marshall Of Somercotes, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Catherine Leeke, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Emma Bingham en 1429, à Hingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1460, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Marshall 1411 in Norbury, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Marshall Of Somercotes 32 und seine Mutter Catherine Leeke 32. Er hat 1429 in Hingham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Emma Bingham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1460 im Alter von 49 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Marshall è nato il nel 1411 nel Norbury, Derbyshire, England, il suo padre, William Marshall Of Somercotes, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Catherine Leeke, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Emma Bingham nel 1429, nel Hingham, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1460, nel England, all'età di 49 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John Marshallが1411年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアノーブリーで生まれた時，父William Marshall Of Somercotesは32歳，母Catherine Leekeは32歳であった。1429年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクヒンガムでElizabeth Emma Binghamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1460年にイギリスで49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Marshall(이)가 1411년 영국 더비실르 노르버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Marshall Of Somercotes(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine Leeke(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1429년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 잉함에서 Elizabeth Emma Bingham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1460년 영국에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Marshall nasceu em 1411, em Norbury, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Marshall Of Somercotes, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Catherine Leeke, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Emma Bingham em 1429, em Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1460, em Inglaterra, com 49 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Marshall родился в 1411 г. в местности норбури, дирбишир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Marshall Of Somercotes было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Catherine Leeke, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Emma Bingham в 1429 г. в местности хингхам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1460 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 49 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Marshall nació en 1411, en Norbury, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Marshall Of Somercotes, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Catherine Leeke, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Emma Bingham en 1429, en Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1460, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 49 años.
